blocks.title: Management (C-Level, Directors)
blocks.audience: Managing director, head of sales and marketing.
role_titles: Chief Operating Officer (COO) / Director of Operations
Chief Commercial Officer (CCO) / Sales & Marketing Director

steps.general: [GENERAL] Ask one opening question: how they define the company's growth strategy in their market and what absolute "red lines" (non-negotiable taboos) exist in their business model. Adapt industry examples to the respondent's stated sector (e.g. metal distribution only if relevant).
steps.deepening: [DEEPENING] Ask how they structure sales and marketing so the organization produces leads aligned with margin strategy—not just volume or "any" leads. Probe concrete mechanisms.
steps.drilling: [DRILLING] Pose a dilemma: a very large strategic customer offers a contract that would massively grow revenue but requires breaking a stated red line (e.g. unacceptable balance-sheet or compliance risk). Ask whether they would refuse, renegotiate, or change the rule—and to justify, in terms credible for their role.

steps.general: [GENERAL] Ask them to describe their decision-making framework when the market is stressed (e.g. commodity price spikes, supply disruption)—worded for their industry context.
steps.deepening: [DEEPENING] Ask for the hardest management decision in roughly the last year tied to financial or operational risk, with enough detail to show real trade-offs.
steps.drilling: [DRILLING] Scenario: a high-impact decision must be made in about two hours with only partial information. Ask how they assess risk and act on the spot—make stakes plausible for their domain.

blocks.title: Sales (Customer-facing experts)
blocks.audience: Technical sales director, KAM, export sales manager.
role_titles: Technical Sales Director / Head of Sales
Export Sales Manager / International Sales Manager

phases.title: Qualifying complex RFQs and early risk signals

steps.general: [GENERAL] Ask about their process for a complex RFQ: which questions they must ask the customer up front before committing resources.
steps.deepening: [DEEPENING] Ask them to explain quoting logic and which early signals (red flags) mean the deal will be toxic and they should walk away.
steps.drilling: [DRILLING] Dilemma: quarter-end, plan is short, a major account sends an RFQ with clear red flags in the spec. Ask whether they pursue it to hit targets and how they balance sales goals vs company risk.

steps.general: [GENERAL] Ask how they read and challenge strict technical specifications from demanding customer segments—adapt examples (medical, aerospace, defense, etc.) to what the respondent actually sells to.
steps.deepening: [DEEPENING] Ask how they translate rigid standard requirements into commercial proposal language that defends price and scope.
steps.drilling: [DRILLING] Ethical tension: they noticed a hidden tolerance or requirement competitors likely missed. Ask whether they disclose it to the customer (possibly lowering price) or use it as an edge—and why.

steps.general: [GENERAL] Ask what is specific or constrained about international shipments of specialty materials in their practice (customs, licenses, classifications)—tied to their markets.
steps.deepening: [DEEPENING] Ask how they screen customers and transactions for sanctions / dual-use / export-control risk.
steps.drilling: [DRILLING] Scenario: goods stuck in a third country after a sudden regulatory change; downstream customer's line stops and penalties are threatened. Ask legal and communication steps in the first 24 hours.

blocks.title: Sales Operations (Operational sales support)
role_titles: Head of Sales Operations / Sales Support Manager
Sales Operations Specialist / Order Management Specialist

steps.general: [GENERAL] Ask what happens after sales confirms with the customer: their process and which ERP or master-data fields are critical so operations and production do not fail.
steps.deepening: [DEEPENING] Ask which mistakes sales typically make when handing off orders and what production or margin consequences follow.
steps.drilling: [DRILLING] They missed a specification error that reached purchasing. Ask who owns financial responsibility in their system and how they resolve the internal conflict without destroying trust.

blocks.title: Procurement (Strategic purchasing and materials)
role_titles: Head of Procurement / Chief Procurement Officer (CPO)
Strategic Purchasing Manager / Global Sourcing Manager

steps.general: [GENERAL] Ask how they evaluate and select mills or strategic suppliers and which country or geopolitical risks they bake into decisions—for their material categories.
steps.deepening: [DEEPENING] Ask how they negotiate when the supplier is effectively sole source for a needed alloy or grade.
steps.drilling: [DRILLING] Scenario: the main low-cost source for a critical material is suddenly unavailable (sanctions, plant shutdown, logistics). Ask for a fast heuristic or playbook to replace supply without quality collapse.

steps.general: [GENERAL] Ask how they handle requests for non-standard or hard-to-source materials.
steps.deepening: [DEEPENING] Ask which rules of thumb they use to propose material equivalents across standards (e.g. ASTM vs EN) while managing customer approval risk.
steps.drilling: [DRILLING] They found a strong technical match but the customer's quality function rejects it for bureaucratic reasons and material is already purchased. Ask how they minimize commercial loss.

blocks.title: Quality & Compliance (Quality management and ESG)
blocks.audience: Head of QM, certification specialist, ESG / LkSG lead.
role_titles: Compliance Officer / ESG & Supply Chain Act (LkSG) Manager

steps.general: [GENERAL] Ask how certificate verification at incoming inspection works in their operation (e.g. EN 10204 3.1 / 3.2 or sector equivalents).
steps.deepening: [DEEPENING] Ask how they ensure traceability from melt or heat through warehouse to customer for their product mix.
steps.drilling: [DRILLING] Crisis: a forged certificate slipped through and the part is already installed at a safety- or audit-sensitive customer. Ask them to walk through protocol including legal and reputational angles.

phases.title: Audit-ready processes and ESG (Supply Chain Act / LkSG)

steps.general: [GENERAL] Ask how they design processes to survive surprise audits from ISO or sector regulators relevant to them.
steps.deepening: [DEEPENING] Ask how they implement supply-chain due diligence (e.g. German Supply Chain Due Diligence Act–style) and assess ESG or human-rights risk among partners.
steps.drilling: [DRILLING] Dilemma: the most profitable supplier violates serious ESG rules; exiting costs a large profit share; staying risks regulator or public backlash. Ask what they decide as compliance lead and why.

blocks.title: Logistics & Operations (Logistics and warehousing)
blocks.audience: Head of logistics, logistics coordinator, warehouse manager.
role_titles: Transport Logistics Coordinator / Freight Forwarding Specialist
Consignment Stock / Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) Manager

steps.general: [GENERAL] Ask how they choose transport modes and build routes for heavy or special cargo in their network.
steps.deepening: [DEEPENING] Ask which tracking signals or carrier communications are early warnings of critical delay.
steps.drilling: [DRILLING] Scenario: accident or loss damages time-critical material; customer is JIT with no buffer. Ask for immediate operational steps they would take as logistics lead.

steps.general: [GENERAL] Ask what logic drives their inventory policy (service, capital, obsolescence) in their context.
steps.deepening: [DEEPENING] Ask how they run consignment or call-off programs when the customer pulls partial lots unpredictably.
steps.drilling: [DRILLING] Scenario: large consignment stock at a customer who enters insolvency; title is yours but goods are on their site. Ask how they recover physically and legally.

phases.title: Process selection and DFM (design for manufacturability)

steps.general: [GENERAL] Ask how they choose processing technology when a new customer drawing arrives (capabilities, cost, risk)—for their shop reality.
steps.deepening: [DEEPENING] Ask which DFM mistakes they most often see from customer designers and how that affects quoting and execution.
steps.drilling: [DRILLING] Sales already committed, but tolerances are not feasible without massive scrap on their equipment. Ask how they negotiate a drawing change without undermining the sales relationship.
